claims.text: CRAFT hand is a tendon-driven anthropomorphic hand with hybrid hard-soft compliance designed for contact-rich manipulation.
claims.source_quote: We introduce CRAFT hand, a tendon-driven anthropomorphic hand with hybrid hard-soft compliance for contact-rich manipulation.

claims.text: Impacts during manipulation concentrate at joints, while links carry most of the load.
claims.source_quote: Impacts concentrate at joints, while links carry most of the load.

claims.text: CRAFT places soft material at joints and keeps links rigid.
claims.source_quote: CRAFT places soft material at joints and keeps links rigid

claims.text: CRAFT uses rolling-contact joint surfaces to keep flexion on repeatable motion paths.
claims.source_quote: uses rolling­contact joint surfaces to keep flexion on repeatable motion paths

claims.text: Fifteen motors mounted on the fingers drive the CRAFT hand through tendons.
claims.source_quote: Fifteen motors mounted on the fingers drive the hand through tendons

claims.text: Mounting motors on the fingers keeps the form factor compact and the fingers light.
claims.source_quote: Fifteen motors mounted on the fingers drive the hand through tendons, keeping the form factor compact and the fingers light.

claims.text: In structural tests, CRAFT improves strength and endurance while maintaining comparable repeatability.
claims.source_quote: In structural tests, CRAFT improves strength and endurance while maintaining comparable repeatability.

claims.text: In teleoperation, CRAFT improves handling of fragile and low-friction items compared to alternatives.
claims.source_quote: In teleoperation, CRAFT improves handling of fragile and low-friction items

claims.text: The CRAFT hand covers 33 out of 33 grasps in the Feix taxonomy.
claims.source_quote: the hand covers 33/33 grasps in the Feix taxonomy

claims.text: The full design of the CRAFT hand costs under $600.
The CRAFT hand design will be released open-source with vision-based teleoperation and simulation integration.
claims.source_quote: will be released open-source with vision­based teleoperation and simulation integration

claims.text: The CRAFT hand design is based on the principle that contact is not uniform across the hand.
claims.source_quote: The design is based on a simple idea: contact is not uniform across the hand.
